author: George Orwell
description: In a totalitarian surveillance state, one man dares to think forbidden thoughts and love a forbidden woman — a chilling prophecy of power, language, and truth

title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
author: Lewis Carroll
description: A young girl falls down a rabbit hole into a fantastical underground world of peculiar creatures, absurd logic, and unforgettable characters

title: Dracula
author: Bram Stoker
description: A vampire count from Transylvania moves to England, preying on the innocent, while a band of heroes fights to stop his reign of terror

title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
description: A young scientist's obsessive quest to create life leads to a terrifying creature and a haunting exploration of ambition, isolation, and what it means to be human

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A tragic tale of love and the American Dream, set in the mysterious world of millionaire Jay Gatsby during the Jazz Age of the 1920s

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A witty tale of love, class, and misjudgment in Regency-era England, following Elizabeth Bennet and the proud Mr. Darcy

title: Romeo and Juliet
author: William Shakespeare
description: Two young lovers from feuding families in Verona defy fate and hatred in Shakespeare's most famous tragedy of passion and sacrifice

title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
description: The world's greatest detective and his loyal companion Dr. Watson solve London's most baffling cases through brilliant deduction and daring adventure
